title: austroblechnum difforme
intro: Austroblechnum difforme, also known as the Rough Water Fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Austroblechnum difforme include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
